_readme: QUIZ TEMPLATE — copy this file, fill it in, and either upload it on the
instructor Quizzes page ('Upload quiz JSON') or drop it in this folder
named <anything>.json and restart the server. Files starting with _ are
ignored by the server, so the template itself never becomes a live quiz.
A full real example is tut1_3d_bar.json next to this file.
- quiz_id: unique id for this quiz. Re-uploading the same quiz_id
replaces its questions (quizzes are not versioned).
- tutorial_id: must be an EXISTING tutorial's id — the quiz attaches
to that tutorial and appears on its page as 'Check your understanding'.
- questions: at least 1; each needs text, 2+ options, and a 0-based
correct_index pointing at the right option.
- concept_tag (recommended): groups questions for the concept-mastery
analytics. Reuse the same short tag across quizzes for the same concept.
- explanation (recommended): shown to the student right after they
answer — say WHY the right answer is right.
- Keys starting with _ are authoring comments and are ignored.
title: Tutorial N — Check your understanding

questions._comment: A concept question. correct_index 1 means option B is right.
questions.text: Why does a static structural model need at least one support before solving?
options: Without a constraint the part can move as a rigid body and the solver has no unique answer
questions.explanation: A static solve finds one equilibrium. An unconstrained body can translate or rotate freely, so the equations have no unique solution until a support anchors it.

questions._comment: A tutorial-specific question — reference the actual numbers/steps students just did.
questions.text: In this tutorial, which result did you insert to read the final answer?
options: The result the tutorial's last section inserts
questions.explanation: Tie the question to the concrete step the student performed, and explain what that result shows.
